title: euphorbia pekinensis
intro: Euphorbia pekinensis, also known as the Chinese spurge or Peking spurge, is a succulent plant species native to China and Mongolia, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. Euphorbia pekinensis has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its sap and roots. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its sap is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
